description:
You are the PCI-DSS Compliance Manager at ShopStream Inc., a fast-growing e-commerce retailer that processes $2.3 billion in annual credit card transactions. A Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) has completed your annual PCI-DSS audit and issued a Report on Compliance with 6 findings requiring formal remediation documentation:

1. PCI-DSS Req 1.2 — Network firewall configuration rules have not been reviewed in over 6 months (High severity)
2. PCI-DSS Req 3.4 — Cardholder data (PAN) is stored unencrypted in the legacy order warehouse database (Critical severity)
3. PCI-DSS Req 6.3 — Web application vulnerability scanning is manual and not performed on every production code release (High severity)
4. PCI-DSS Req 7.2 — Privileged administrator access reviews have not been conducted in the past 12 months (High severity)
5. PCI-DSS Req 10.6 — Security log review is entirely manual and relies on a single analyst with no backup coverage (Medium severity)
6. PCI-DSS Req 12.10 — The incident response plan has not been tested or exercised in 18 months (High severity)

Using Eramba (credentials at /home/ga/eramba/credentials.txt), document the remediation of these findings. Create risks for all 6 findings with a 'Mitigate' treatment strategy. Establish at least 3 internal controls (Security Services) addressing the highest-severity findings (firewall configuration audit procedure, PAN encryption enforcement, and privileged access review program). Initiate a project with 'PCI' in the title to track the remediation sprint. Document formal policy exceptions for the unencrypted legacy warehouse database (Req 3.4) and the manual log review process (Req 10.6), ensuring expiration dates are no more than 6 months out. Finally, create a security policy titled 'Payment Card Data Security Policy' with a 'Draft' status.
